# cozy

> Animated rain over your Wayland wallpaper. cozy is a `wlr-layer-shell` client
> that renders your wallpaper and composites animated rain on top of it — glass
> droplets that refract the wallpaper behind them — using OpenGL ES via EGL.
> Clicks fall through to the desktop. Linux/Wayland only; written in Rust.

In the default opaque mode cozy *owns* the wallpaper (it must, to refract those
pixels), so it runs instead of a wallpaper daemon. An `--overlay` mode renders
transparent rain (premultiplied alpha, per-effect coverage) *alongside* an
existing swww/hyprpaper daemon. Rain is a swappable "effect" with a shared
shader uniform contract; effects are switched live over a Unix control socket.

## Engine (Rust)

- [src/main.rs](src/main.rs): bootstrap + CLI — run the daemon, or send a control command (set / effect / weather)
- [src/control.rs](src/control.rs): Unix-socket control protocol
- [src/app.rs](src/app.rs): app state + all Wayland event handlers; layer-surface creation, opaque region
- [src/surface.rs](src/surface.rs): one Background layer surface per output, plus its drawing
- [src/render/egl.rs](src/render/egl.rs): EGL display/context setup on a Wayland surface
- [src/render/gl.rs](src/render/gl.rs): effect registry (`EFFECTS`), fullscreen-triangle draw, uniforms
- [src/render/texture.rs](src/render/texture.rs): decode an image into a mipmapped RGBA8 GL texture

## Shaders

- [shaders/rain.vert](shaders/rain.vert): fullscreen triangle
- [shaders/effects/](shaders/effects/): one fragment shader per effect — `droplet` (rain on glass, ported from Heartfelt; CC BY-NC-SA), `ripple` (water surface, MIT), `snow` (parallax snow, ported from Just Snow; CC BY-NC-SA), `clouds` (drifting fractal clouds, ported from 2D Clouds; CC BY-NC-SA). Each honors `u_resolution`, `u_tex_resolution`, `u_wallpaper`, `u_time`, `u_wind`, `u_intensity`, `u_overlay` (and defines its own coverage alpha for overlay mode).

## Integrations (install glue — one binary, three profiles)

- [install.sh](install.sh): top-level dispatcher — detect setup, confirm, delegate (`COZY_INTEGRATION` to skip the prompt)
- [integrations/common.sh](integrations/common.sh): shared installer machinery (preflight, fetch, build, install binary)
- [integrations/caelestia/](integrations/caelestia/): cozy owns the wallpaper, wired into Caelestia (systemd unit, postHook, shell.json)
- [integrations/standalone/](integrations/standalone/): cozy owns the wallpaper on plain Hyprland (exec-once, cozy.conf, cozy-wall)
- [integrations/swww-overlay/](integrations/swww-overlay/): cozy alongside swww/hyprpaper via `--overlay` (transparent rain overlay)

## Docs

- [README.md](README.md): install + usage + architecture
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): build / lint / verify and conventions for coding agents
- [docs/superpowers/specs/](docs/superpowers/specs/): design specs (e.g. the multi-integration monorepo + overlay-mode design)

## Build & verify

- Build: `cargo build --release` (Linux + Wayland/EGL dev headers)
- The dev machine is macOS but cozy is Linux-only, so it is verified in a Linux container: `make verify` (headless sway + Mesa software renderer + grim screenshots into `./out/`), `make lint` (rustfmt + clippy).
